Madame Giry Review: The Matron of Dancers Strategy Guide

Madame Giry dances into the arena with a skill set that screams 'tactical nuke' wrapped in a slow mana package. With fiends that absorb massive healing and block buffs, she's a nightmare for stall teams—if you can keep her alive long enough to fire. We break down her role in the modern meta.
Madame Giry: The Matron of Dancers
Madame Giry, The Matron of Dancers: A Slow Burn of Fiends and Shadows
Madame Giry is a 5-star Legendary Dark hero from the
event. She enters the stage as a

mana Sorcerer (Wizard in some iterations, but her card says Wizard) who serves a very specific purpose: total attrition warfare. Released in late 2023, she has faced the harsh winds of power creep, but her kit remains mechanically unique enough to warrant a look. She is essentially the "Lu Bu" of the Opera, designed to shut down enemy sustain and buffs permanently until her "gifts" are dealt with.

The Matron of Dancers cuts a striking figure. Unlike the usual high-fantasy armor or monstrous visages, she is dressed in severe, elegant Victorian-era attire, holding a lantern that presumably lights the way to your team's demise. The art captures her stern, authoritarian nature perfectly—she isn't here to fight you; she's here to discipline you. The "Ballerina Fiends" she summons are a delightfully creepy touch, adding a layer of psychological horror to the battle.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Opera family is all about stacking stats, which she desperately needs.

The Opera family bonus grants increased Attack, Defense, and Health for each unique family member on the team. This helps patch her stats slightly if you run her with newer, stronger Opera heroes (like updated versions or costumes if they exist).
She gains 20% health when a status ailment on her expires or is cleansed. This is a vital survival tool, helping her stay alive against DOT-heavy teams.
She is immune to attack down. This ensures that when she finally does fire, her fiends land with their full attack value (since fiend damage scales off the caster's attack).
is... okay. It fits her theme, but practically, you'd prefer something like
to help her survive or hit harder. It helps her in mirror matches or against the Goblin meta, but it's not a game-changer.

Upon release, Madame Giry was seen as a "Lu Bu 2.0" – a slow but devastating fiend summoner. She received a buff shortly after release (damage increased from ~300% to 340%, fiend absorption increased), which made her viable in Rush formats.
The mechanic is timeless (buff block + heal absorb), but the delivery system (Slow speed, low stats) is outdated. Modern heroes do "Hit All + nasty effect" at Average or even Fast speed now.
In standard Raid or War formats, she is too slow and too fragile. She will die before she fires. However, in
, she is still a terrifying force. If she fires in Rush, the enemy team usually cannot recover.
You are using Madame Giry for the effect, not the direct damage. She needs to survive to fire.
Maximizes her chances of surviving until her mana bar fills. Every point of defense helps against modern 1500+ Attack stats.
Her initial hit will be softer, and her fiend tick damage (based on attack) will be slightly lower.
Increases the initial hit and, crucially, the recurring damage of the fiends.
She is extremely fragile and likely won't survive to use her skill in today's high-damage meta.
path is recommended. If she doesn't fire, she does nothing. Dead heroes deal zero damage.
The combination of Buff Immunity and huge Healing Absorption completely neuters support heroes.
In Very Fast mana modes, she is an S-tier threat.
-44% Dark Defense Down pairs beautifully with faster Dark snipers or hit-all heroes.
Slow mana is a death sentence in the current fast-paced meta without mana acceleration support.
Her defensive stats are paper-thin compared to 2026 heroes.

She needs bodyguards. Do not leave her exposed.
Ludwig (or a modern taunter) buys time. If Ludwig fires and charges Giry, it's game over.
Use mana boosters to artificially increase her speed.
Hansel, Gretel, Proteus, or modern equivalents who stop her from ever firing.
If a Taunter fires before her, she only hits the taunter.
If your team fills the enemy board with minions (e.g., Bera), there might be no space for her Fiends.
: Fiends displace minions, but if the enemy has 3 minions, the fiend just pushes one out. It's usually fine.

If you pulled her early, she is a fantastic "boss killer" for map stages. Her fiends will chew through high-HP bosses while preventing them from buffing. Level her, but be aware she will struggle in Raids against faster teams.

She is a luxury tool for your 4th or 5th War team, specifically for Rush wars. Do not Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether) her unless you have an excess of materials and love the art. Her stats are just too far behind to justify the premium resources compared to a modern Construct or Astral hero.
Many players initially compared her to Lu Bu. One user noted,
"She is Lu Bu 1.5 basically… not bad but not as good as the other operas."
Post-release, she received a buff that changed the narrative.
"She was buffed in damage, her minions deal way more damage then was in beta... she is more then great,"
A more recent take from 2024 sums it up:
"Just landed her... she’s stuck at 3/70 along with 10+ other heroes... stats are 4

Madame Giry is the strict headmistress who is sadly teaching at a school that has been closed for renovations. Her curriculum (skill set) is excellent—fiends, buff block, defense down—but the facility (stats and speed) is crumbling. She is a devastating force in Rush wars and a fun toy for PvE, but in the cutthroat world of high-level competitive play, she has largely danced her last number.
A niche powerhouse for Rush modes, but keep her on the bench for standard play.
